blocked_moves: treating WARN-SIE-073 as execution authority rather than advisory warning
relying on product family matching without exact license evidence
assuming license applies to any destination when it specifies Peru
treating planning artifact or general policy as substitute for specific license
authorizing release without resolving destination mismatch
dependency_ledger: General warning (WARN-SIE-073) provides advisory context but cannot override explicit license scope. License (LIC-SIE-073) specifies Peru as destination. Shipment (SHIP-SIE-073) specifies Chile as destination. Policy (STD-SIE-073-P) requires exact match on all five fields (ECCN, destination, end user, shipment date, effective date). Mismatch on destination blocks closure. No current source bridges the gap between Peru-licensed and Chile-intended shipment.
gov_command: Diagnose previous worker output, preserve correct source bindings, repair gate failures, block invalid moves, and create the next worker baton.
must_preserve: destination mismatch finding that blocks release
LIC-SIE-073 license scope as authoritative limiting document
SHIP-SIE-073 as the actual shipment fact record
must_repair: destination mismatch between license scope (Peru) and shipment destination (Chile)
ambiguity about whether license could be extended or shipment redirected
unresolved_dependency: General warning versus exact export execution license tension

next_worker_baton.attack_focus: Adversarial stress-test of license scope versus shipment destination: attempt to find any valid bridge (amended license, redirected shipment, corrected documentation) that could close the boundary, or definitively confirm no such bridge exists under strict exact-match rules.
monotonic_preservation: License scope limitation to Peru per LIC-SIE-073
Shipment destination as Chile per SHIP-SIE-073
Warning against product-family-only matching per WARN-SIE-073
Verification that no bridge exists between Peru license and Chile shipment under current evidence
next_worker_baton.objective: Perform adversarial cross-DNA verification to confirm destination mismatch cannot be resolved under current license scope, and identify required repairs for closure.
required_repairs: Obtain amended export license that explicitly covers Chile destination instead of/in addition to Peru
OR redirect shipment SHIP-SIE-073 to Peru (matching LIC-SIE-073 destination)
OR obtain superseding license document that covers both Peru and Chile destinations
OR void SHIP-SIE-073 and reissue with corrected destination matching existing license

source_gate_interpretation: Gate PASSED with ESCALATE verdict. No local gate failures. SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN binding correctly preserved. Destination mismatch (Peru vs Chile) identified as open blocker per exact-match policy STD-SIE-073-P.
